title: What brands are paying to advertise around the World Cup
description: One in three Americans is set to watch the World Cup this summer, leading many brands to look into last-minute partnerships, proximity marketing initiatives and social campaigns tied to the tournament. Official partnerships are pricey, but there are still opportunities across host cities for brands seeking a timely halo effect. Modern Retail sat down with media buying experts to learn more.

title: Fox One pitches itself as home of World Cup viewing for obsessive fans
description: The DTC service looks to break through in streaming with a campaign that plays up the absurd lengths fans will go to watch the tournament.

title: How Reddit Drives GenAI Visibility
description:
Reddit is ChatGPT's leading source of answers, whether drawn from training data or pulled from live web searches.
The post How Reddit Drives GenAI Visibility appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Amazon launches AI image generator to narrow search queries
description: Users describing a product in the e-commerce giant’s app can now choose from a variety of AI-generated images to shop similar items.

title: Best Buy rolls out Meta Lab shop-in-shops
description: The retailer will debut the spaces — which allow shoppers to test Meta's lineup of AI glasses and VR headsets — at 50 locations this summer.

title: Delivery reliability trumps speed, Macy’s and Ulta execs say
description: Maintaining on-time delivery promises will keep shoppers coming back, but fast shipping still has a role to play, retailers said at Home Delivery World 2026.

title: Amazon’s Alexa for Shopping adds customization feature for merch
description:
Amazon is continuing to expand the role of generative artificial intelligence (AI) in its shopper experiences, enabling new customization features through Alexa for Shopping. Consumers will be able to create custom designs for merch — including T-shirts, sweatshirts and water bottles — by describing an idea to Amazon’s AI-powered shopping assistant. Amazon said it plans […]
The post Amazon’s Alexa for Shopping adds customization feature for merch appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: How to use your CRM for smarter email marketing campaigns
description: Customer relationship management (CRM) systems have become foundational to effective email marketing. For teams learning how to use a CRM for email marketing, the key is connecting contact data, segmentation, automation, and measurement into a single, cohesive workflow.

title: Amazon now lets you design custom merch using AI
description: A new feature in the Amazon Shopping app allows users to generate designs with Alexa, then print them on products like T-shirts, hoodies, and tumblers.

title: EXCLUSIVE: Accenture Song Will Buy Whalar, Gaining Global Scale in Influencer Marketing
description: Accenture Song, the consulting giant's marketing services arm, will acquire marketing agency Whalar.

title: WPP puts a different type of ad talent to the test with new studio Hex
description: Made up mostly of Gen Zers with backgrounds outside of traditional advertising, Hex aims to address the industry’s AI skills gap.

title: Why the time is now for experiential marketing — and how to get it right
description: Melissa Levy, president of experiential marketing agency Sparks, explains the channel’s rise and how marketers can navigate its growing pains.

title: Why Tropicana is rejuvenating its creative but keeping the same strategy
description: CMO Chris Tussing explains how its new campaign is a creative evolution and demonstrates what the brand has learned about effective media planning.

title: Stop treating search and video as separate teams
description:
The strongest campaigns connect creative, media, search, and measurement into a single demand generation strategy.
The post Stop treating search and video as separate teams appeared first on MarTech.

title: How AI is turning lead scoring into a decision engine
description:
Predictive models and real-time signals are replacing static scoring, helping sales focus on leads most likely to convert.
The post How AI is turning lead scoring into a decision engine appeared first on MarTech.
